today_summary: GCM26 settled $4,082.5 in the most recent session, unchanged for the week after round-tripping through a $4,167.4 high. Call-heavy chain (P/C OI 0.57) with call OI 136,300 nearly double put OI 77,748; nearest support is the $4,000 put wall (4,128 contracts, -2.0% below spot) and resistance is stacked at $4,500 / $4,700 / $5,100. Max pain $4,770 sits $687.5 (+16.8%) above spot, a distant pull given the 20-day DTE. ATM IV 21.32% implies a 1-sigma daily move of $54.8 (1.3%).
five_day_thesis: Spot round-tripped the full week — $4,082.5 to $4,126.1 to $4,167.4 to $4,157.3 back to $4,082.5 — netting to zero, but the dealer gamma regime flipped twice underneath that flat print: positive GEX (+8.0 to +62.9) held Jul 3-8 as spot rallied into the $4,167 high, then flipped back to negative (-51.3) today as spot fell back to the week's open, mirroring the negative reading from Jul 2 (-60.1). Max pain drifted down $25 ($4,795 to $4,770) while the $4,000 put wall and $5,370 far-dated call wall stayed pinned all 5 sessions — OI structure is static even as price round-tripped $85. ATM IV compressed sharply, -2.85pp (24.17% to 21.32%), so realized range this week failed to keep pace with priced vol, a setup that typically favors premium sellers unless the negative-GEX regime reasserts itself. Net read: dealers were long gamma (dampening) into the midweek rally and are short gamma (amplifying) again at the week's close — watch for a volatility pickup on a break of either range boundary.
what_changed: GEX sign flipped negative (-51.3) today, reversing 3 sessions of positive dealer gamma that peaked +62.9 on Jul 7
Spot round-tripped: $4,082.5 to a $4,167.4 high (Jul 7) back to $4,082.5, net unchanged for the week
ATM IV compressed 24.17% to 21.32% (-2.85pp), the sharpest 5-day drop in the window
Max pain drifted lower $4,795 to $4,770 (-$25), widening its premium over spot to +16.8%
Put wall ($4,000, 4,128 OI) and call wall ($5,370, 8,008 OI) unchanged all 5 sessions
P/C OI ratio steady at 0.57 all week — no shift in directional positioning despite the price round trip
prediction: Bias neutral-to-cautious inside the $4,000-$4,167 range that has bounded the last 5 sessions. A reclaim of $4,167 with GEX flipping back positive favors a grind toward the $4,500 call cluster; a break of the $4,000 put wall reactivates the negative-GEX cascade last seen Jul 2 and opens $3,900/$3,600. Close above $4,167 flips bias bullish, close below $4,000 flips it bearish.

day_trade_cta.bias_note: Spot $4,082.5 sits mid-range between the $4,000 put wall and the $4,167 weekly high; today's GEX flip to negative (-51.3) argues for range expansion, not a clean directional edge.
day_trade_cta.entry_note: Scale in near the put-wall test; avoid chasing mid-range with IV already crushed to 21.3%.
day_trade_cta.pivot_note: Reclaim and hold above targets $4,300 then the $4,500 call cluster. Rejection here keeps price capped inside the weekly range.
day_trade_cta.stop_note: -2.6% below entry midpoint; a clean break of the $4,000 put wall confirms the negative-GEX cascade and invalidates the range thesis.
day_trade_cta.target_note: T1 is the week's high, take partial. T2/T3 sit at rising call gamma ($4,300 net GEX +27.2, $4,500 the next major OI wall).
day_trade_cta.risk_label: GEX SIGN FLIP - WATCH VOL PICKUP
day_trade_cta.risk_note: Dealer gamma flipped negative today after 3 sessions positive; 1-sigma daily move is $54.8 (1.3%). Reduce size until the regime confirms one direction.
